title: VA SAC vs pragmatic M standard compliance
description: Joins the VA Standards and Conventions overlay (mappings/va-sac.tsv) against the pragmatic M standard (integrated/pragmatic-m-standard.tsv) and surfaces portability concerns for VistA developers. See docs/va-sac-and-pragmatic-standard.md for the full rationale.

aligned.description: SAC and pragmatic agree: SAC permits a portable entry OR SAC forbids an unportable entry.

sac_says_use_but_not_portable.description: SAC permits/recommends an entry that's NOT in pragmatic-core. VistA code following SAC literally would break on the missing engine.

sac_says_avoid_but_portable.description: SAC forbids/restricts an entry that IS in pragmatic-core. SAC may be overly conservative, or the entry was added to both engines after SAC was written.

sac_silent_on_portable.description: SAC doesn't mention an entry that's pragmatic-core. Probably safe but unguided.

sac_silent_on_unportable.description: SAC doesn't mention an entry that's vendor-specific. Likely fine for VistA developers to ignore.
